title: Class-aware next-stop finish margin
summary: Add slick-versus-wet-weather next-stop finish-margin features to 001214.
rationale: This patch separates whether a fresh slick or wet-weather tyre could reach the finish after the next stop. This is a small domain block close to pit-stop strategy and less invasive than full finishability or legality state.
implementation_hint: Start from 001214 exactly. Add only the class-aware next-stop margin block using the formulas below: define `Is_Slick = Compound in {SOFT, MEDIUM, HARD}`, `Is_WetWeather = Compound in {INTERMEDIATE, WET}`, `fresh_slick_life = 31.0`, `fresh_wetweather_life = 22.0`, `slick_next_stop_finish_margin = fresh_slick_life - remaining_laps_est`, `wetweather_next_stop_finish_margin = fresh_wetweather_life - remaining_laps_est`. Add `class_aware_next_stop_finish_margin = select(Is_Slick, slick_margin; Is_WetWeather, wetweather_margin; default=max(slick_margin, wetweather_margin))`, `class_aware_next_stop_can_finish = class_aware_margin >= 0 and remaining_laps_est > 0`, `mixed_class_switch_to_wetweather = Is_Slick & wetweather_margin >= 0 & remaining_laps_est > 0`, `mixed_class_switch_to_slick = Is_WetWeather & slick_margin >= 0 & remaining_laps_est > 0`, `mixed_class_switch_margin = select(Is_Slick, wetweather_margin; Is_WetWeather, slick_margin; default=0)`, `current_class_next_stop_margin = select(Is_Slick, slick_margin; Is_WetWeather, wetweather_margin; default=0)`, and `current_class_next_stop_can_finish = current_class_next_stop_margin >= 0 and remaining_laps_est > 0`. Do not add ID rarity in this patch.
expected_effect: Improve rows where pit urgency depends on whether the next tyre class can finish the race.
risk: The fixed 31/22 lap class-life assumptions may be too coarse and can conflict with more detailed compound features already in 001214.
